commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: fix(speech): honor task cancellation in recorder poll loop
target.body:
## Summary

The `observeRecorderState()` poll loop in `Speech.swift` uses `try? await Task.sleep(nanoseconds:)` to throttle polling. The `try?` silently discards `CancellationError`, causing the loop to run indefinitely when the parent task is cancelled.

## Problem

When the speech recognizer's parent task is cancelled (e.g., view dismissed or app entering background), the `observeRecorderState` loop continues polling the recorder's `isRecording` and `transcript` state every 100ms. The loop only exits when `self.isListening` becomes false or `recorder.isRecording` becomes false, but neither is set by task cancellation.

The `try? await Task.sleep` pattern swallows `CancellationError` along with other errors. Without an explicit cancellation check, the cooperative cancellation contract of Swift Structured Concurrency is broken.

## Fix

Add `guard !Task.isCancelled else { break }` after the `try? await Task.sleep` call. This is the standard Swift pattern for honoring cooperative cancellation in poll loops that use `try?`.

The guard is a no-op during normal execution and only triggers when the task has been explicitly cancelled by its parent.

## Verification

- `swift build --package-path Apps/Mac`: Build complete (0 errors)
- `git diff --check`: clean
- This is the same pattern applied in #193 (merged), which fixed an identical bug in `WatchCaptureSession`

## Origin

The poll loop was introduced in commit `babc87d58` (2025-07-30, Peter Steinberger) as part of the original speech recognizer implementation, approximately 11 months ago.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** The `observeRecorderState()` poll loop in `Speech.swift` ignores task cancellation because `try? await Task.sleep` silently discards `CancellationError`. Dismissing the speech view or the app entering background leaves this loop running indefinitely.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 26, Swift 6.3.3 (swiftlang-6.3.3.1.3), built from patched source on `fix/speech-poll-cancellation` branch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```bash
  cd /Users/sebastientardif/Peekaboo
  git checkout fix/speech-poll-cancellation
  swift build --package-path Apps/Mac
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the patched build:

  ```console
  $ swift build --package-path Apps/Mac
  Build complete!
  ```

  Guard placement verified:

  ```console
  $ grep -n 'Task.isCancelled' Apps/Mac/Peekaboo/Core/Speech.swift
  301:            guard !Task.isCancelled else { break }
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Build succeeds with the cancellation guard compiled into the recorder poll loop. The guard breaks the loop immediately when the parent task is cancelled, instead of continuing to poll indefinitely.
- **What was not tested:** Live cancellation with active speech recognition (requires microphone access and SFSpeechRecognizer authorization). The fix is identical in structure to the pattern merged in #193.

## Related

- Same bug class fixed in #193 (merged): `fix(capture): honor stop during watch transient backoff`
- Cancellation guards in `waitForImage`: commits `dd6d47fd`, `dc75fe4f`
target.author: sebtardif
